history.title: Japanese chemical giant JSR expands to Taiwan for EUV photoresist production near TSMC — plant to fill missing chemical link to scale EUV materials
history.description: The plant, located in Yunlin County, is expected to come online as early as 2028 and will co-develop advanced photoresists with TSMC.
history.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter As chipmakers push EUV lithography toward its physical limits at 2nm and below, the advanced chemicals used to pattern those circuits have become a critical bottleneck. Photoresists, the light-sensitive materials that transfer circuit designs onto silicon wafers, must be reformulated for each new process node, and the most advanced EUV-grade resists are produced almost exclusively by a handful of Japanese suppliers. With AI chip demand driving record orders at leading foundries, those suppliers are now racing to build production capacity closer to their biggest customers.JSR, the Japanese chemical maker that holds roughly a quarter of the global photoresist market, established a joint venture with Taiwanese partners Wah Lee Industrial and LCY Chemical in early April to build its first photoresist production facility in Taiwan. The plant, located in Yunlin County, is expected to come online as early as 2028 and will co-develop advanced photoresists with TSMC, ending the company's status as the last of Japan's three leading EUV-class resist suppliers without a Taiwanese manufacturing base.The expansion comes as JSR simultaneously ramps the world's first production-scale facility for metal oxide resist (MOR) in South Korea, a next-gen EUV chemistry that the company acquired through its $514 million purchase of Inpria in 2021. Together, the two plants represent a coordinated push to lock JSR into the development pipelines of the world's most important chipmakers before Chinese competitors can close the gap at the leading edge.Latest Videos From You may like JSR to build first Taiwan photoresist plant to co-develop advanced resists with TSMC Imec's new post-exposure bake method speeds up EUV chipmaking tools, boosting production for the most advanced chips IBM and Lam's new partnership paves the way toward sub-1nm logic using High-NA EUV JSR under new ownershipJSR is no longer a publicly traded company. Japan Investment Corporation (JIC), a government-backed fund, completed a tender offer at ¥4,350 per share in April 2024, securing more than 84% of outstanding stock. JSR delisted from the Tokyo Stock Exchange on June 25, 2024, and the merger was finalized in December. The deal valued the company at roughly ¥909 billion ($6.4 billion).Under JIC's ownership, JSR has moved aggressively to concentrate on semiconductor materials. The company divested non-core assets to Resonac and Tokuyama in early 2025 and exited its biotech business entirely. In May 2024, it acquired Kyoto-based Yamanaka Hutech, adding chemical vapor deposition (CVD) and atomic layer deposition (ALD) precursor expertise. Then, in September last year, JSR settled long-running patent litigation with Lam Research and converted it into a cross-licensing agreement covering dry-resist EUV patterning and etch precursors.JSR's decision was driven by a direct request from TSMC, according to CommonWealth Magazine. New CEO Tetsuro Hori, who took over in April 2025, told the publication that "speed is critical," noting that local production would eliminate the need to ship wafers out of Taiwan during co-development cycles.JSR had been shipping resist samples from facilities in Japan, the U.S., and Belgium, with each development cycle taking weeks for round-trip shipping alone. A week after the joint venture was announced, JSR opened a separate advanced planarization research center in Hukou, Hsinchu County, in partnership with TSMC and Applied Materials.Location, location, locationA Photoresist is the light-sensitive material that transfers circuit patterns onto silicon wafers during lithography. At advanced process nodes, resist formulations need to be precisely tuned to work with specific exposure wavelengths, dose profiles, etch chemistries, and integration workflows. Each new node requires hundreds of iterative test cycles between the resist supplier and the foundry.JSR's two largest Japanese rivals have had a co-development presence in Taiwan for years. Tokyo Ohka Kogyo (TOK) and Shin-Etsu Chemical, the first- and third-largest photoresist suppliers respectively, both operate production facilities on the island where their engineers work directly alongside TSMC's process teams.Shin-Etsu runs a line in Douliu, also in Yunlin County, and is building a new ¥83 billion facility in Isesaki, Gunma Prefecture, while TOK has been present in Taiwan for more than a decade and announced a ¥20 billion photoresist plant in South Korea in late 2025 to serve Samsung. This means that every major Japanese materials supplier is now either manufacturing in Taiwan or actively building out capacity to do so. What to read next ASML makes breakthrough in EUV chipmaking tech, plans to increase speed by 50% by 2030 ASML's roadmap for chipmaking lithography tools examined — from DUV to Low-NA, High-NA, Hyper-NA, and beyond Rapidus secures $1.7 billion from Japan’s government and private investors for 2nm chip production JSR's Taiwan plant will produce photoresist for TSMC, but in the longer term, the company will focus on metal oxide resist (MOR). MOR uses tin-oxide-based chemistry rather than the organic polymers and photoacid generators found in CARs, which rely on chemical amplification to compensate for the few high-energy photons produced by the light source at 13.5nm EUV wavelengths. However, that amplification introduces acid-diffusion blur and worsening line-edge roughness as feature sizes shrink.Tin-oxide MOR absorbs EUV photons roughly five times more efficiently than organic CARs, according to Inpria, and uses molecular building blocks roughly five times smaller, while etch resistance is 10 to 100 times higher. At SPIE Advanced Lithography 2025, Inpria reported MOR patterning down to pitch-18 with full etch transfer, while Imec demonstrated additional dose-response improvements by adjusting oxygen concentration during the post-exposure bake step.JSR's MOR production plant in Cheongju, South Korea, built through its JSR Micro Korea subsidiary, is expected to begin mass production this year, supplying Samsung Electronics and SK hynix with tin-based MOR for EUV layers in next-gen DRAM. Both memory makers are reportedly planning to adopt MOR on selected layers for their 1c (sixth-gen 10nm-class) DRAM nodes.JSR plans to market MOR to TSMC as well, according to Nikkei. TSMC has stated repeatedly that it won’t adopt high-NA EUV through its A14 (1.4nm-class) node in 2028, instead extending low-NA with multi-patterning, which pushes the largest MOR opportunity at TSMC's logic fabs out toward 1.0nm-class processes and beyond.Chinese competition at the lower endJapanese companies collectively control roughly 80% of the global photoresist market, and dominance at the EUV level is even more concentrated: JSR, TOK, and Shin-Etsu account for nearly 85% of EUV resist production volume, according to industry estimates. Chinese firms have made progress at the KrF and i-line level, but penetration at ArF and above remains negligible. Domestic Chinese supply of ArF and EUV resist sits below 5%.The names to watch are Hubei Dinglong, Xuzhou B&C Chemical (backed by Huawei's Hubble Investment arm), Jiangsu Nata Optoelectronic, and Shanghai Sinyang. Xuzhou B&C claimed it achieved a 14nm wet-process photoresist breakthrough in 2024 and targets advanced mass production within five years, according to TrendForce, but analysts view that timeline as optimistic given the multi-year customer-qualification cycles that resist adoption requires."Chinese players are a threat, but it'll still be some time before they can catch up with us and take market share," Toru Kimura, a senior officer at JSR who heads the company's electronic materials business, told Nikkei. Specific capacity figures, the output mix between MOR and conventional resists, and the exact scope of the Yunlin plant haven’t been disclosed.
history.author: Luke James
history.titleZh: 日本化学巨头JSR在台积电附近扩大台湾业务以生产EUV光刻胶——该工厂将填补扩展EUV材料所需的化学环节缺失
history.descriptionZh:
随着芯片制造商将EUV光刻技术推向2nm及以下节点的物理极限，用于绘制电路图案的先进化学品已成为关键瓶颈。光刻胶——将电路设计转移到硅晶圆上的光敏材料——必须针对每个新工艺节点重新配方，而最高端的EUV级光刻胶几乎完全由少数几家日本供应商生产。在AI芯片需求推动领先代工厂创下订单纪录的背景下，这些供应商正竞相将产能建设在离最大客户更近的地方。

日本化学制造商JSR（全球光刻胶市场份额约四分之一）于4月初与台湾合作伙伴华立企业（Wah Lee Industrial）和长春化工（LCY Chemical）成立合资企业，在台湾建设其首座光刻胶生产工厂。该工厂位于云林县，预计最早于2028年投产，并将与台积电共同开发先进光刻胶。这使得JSR成为日本三大EUV级光刻胶供应商中最后一家在台湾建立生产基地的企业。

此次扩张的同时，JSR还在韩国启动了全球首座规模化金属氧化物光刻胶（MOR）生产设施——这种下一代EUV化学品是JSR于2021年以5.14亿美元收购Inpria获得的。这两座工厂构成了协同布局，旨在在中国竞争对手缩小与前沿差距之前，将JSR锁定在全球最重要芯片制造商的开发管线中。

### JSR的新所有权结构

JSR已不再是上市公司。日本政府支持的基金——日本产业革新投资机构（JIC）于2024年4月完成以每股4350日元的要约收购，获得超过84%的流通股。JSR于2024年6月25日从东京证券交易所退市，并于12月完成合并。该交易对公司的估值约为9090亿日元（约64亿美元）。

在JIC旗下，JSR积极聚焦半导体材料业务。2025年初，公司将非核心资产出售给昭和电工（Resonac）和德山（Tokuyama），并完全退出生物技术业务。2024年5月，JSR收购了京都的Yamanaka Hutech，获得了化学气相沉积（CVD）和原子层沉积（ALD）前驱体技术。随后在去年9月，JSR与泛林半导体（Lam Research）解决了长期专利诉讼，并转化为涵盖干式光刻胶EUV图案化和蚀刻前驱体的交叉许可协议。

据《天下杂志》报道，JSR的决定源于台积电的直接要求。2025年4月上任的新CEO堀哲郎（Tetsuro Hori）告诉该杂志：“速度至关重要。”他指出，本地化生产将消除在共同开发周期中将晶圆运出台湾的需求。此前，JSR从日本、美国和比利时的工厂运送光刻胶样品，每个开发周期仅往返运输就需数周。合资企业宣布一周后，JSR在台湾新竹县湖口镇与台积电、应用材料合作开设了一个独立的先进平坦化研究中心。

### 地理位置的重要性

光刻胶是光刻过程中将电路图案转移到硅晶圆上的光敏材料。在先进工艺节点，光刻胶配方需要针对特定的曝光波长、剂量分布、刻蚀化学和集成流程进行精确调整。每个新节点都需要在光刻胶供应商和代工厂之间进行数百次迭代测试周期。

JSR的两个最大的日本竞争对手在台湾进行联合开发已有多年。东京应化工业（TOK）和信越化学（Shin-Etsu Chemical）——分别为第一和第三大光刻胶供应商——均在台湾运营生产设施，其工程师直接与台积电的工艺团队合作。信越在云林县斗六市有一条生产线，并在群马县伊势崎市建设一座新的830亿日元工厂；TOK在台湾已有十多年历史，并于2025年底宣布在韩国建设200亿日元的光刻胶工厂，以服务三星。这意味着所有主要日本材料供应商现在要么在台湾生产，要么正在积极扩大产能。

JSR的台湾工厂将生产用于台积电的光刻胶，但长期来看，公司将专注于金属氧化物光刻胶（MOR）。MOR采用基于氧化锡的化学物质，而非传统化学放大光刻胶（CAR）中的有机聚合物和光酸产生剂。CAR依赖化学放大来补偿13.5nm EUV波长光源产生的高能光子不足，但这种放大过程会引入酸扩散模糊，随着特征尺寸缩小，线条边缘粗糙度恶化。

根据Inpria的数据，氧化锡MOR吸收EUV光子的效率大约是有机CAR的五倍，使用的分子构建块尺寸也小约五倍，而抗刻蚀性则高出10到100倍。在2025年SPIE先进光刻会议上，Inpria报告了MOR成功实现间距18nm（pitch-18）的图案化并完成完整刻蚀转移，而imec则通过调整后曝光烘烤步骤中的氧浓度展示出额外的剂量响应改进。

JSR在韩国清州的MOR生产工厂通过子公司JSR Micro Korea建设，预计今年开始量产，为三星电子和SK海力士提供基于锡的MOR，用于下一代DRAM的EUV层。据报道，两家存储器制造商都计划在1c（第六代10nm级）DRAM节点的选定层上采用MOR。据日经报道，JSR还计划向台积电推销MOR。台积电多次表示，在2028年的A14（1.4nm级）节点上不会采用高数值孔径（high-NA）EUV，而是通过多重图案化扩展低数值孔径（low-NA），这将把MOR在台积电逻辑晶圆厂的最大应用机会推至1.0nm级工艺及更后。

### 低端市场的中国竞争

日本公司合计控制全球约80%的光刻胶市场，而在EUV级别的统治力更为集中：据行业估计，JSR、TOK和信越占据了近85%的EUV光刻胶产量。中国企业在KrF和i-line级别取得了进展，但在ArF及以上的渗透率仍微不足道。中国本土ArF和EUV光刻胶的供应量低于5%。

值得关注的中国公司包括湖北鼎龙、徐州博康（由华为旗下哈勃投资支持）、江苏南大光电和上海新阳。据TrendForce报道，徐州博康声称2024年实现了14nm湿法光刻胶的突破，并计划在五年内实现先进量产。但分析师认为，考虑到光刻胶采用所需的多年客户验证周期，这一时间表过于乐观。

JSR负责电子材料业务的高级官员木村彻（Toru Kimura）告诉日经：“中国玩家是一个威胁，但他们要赶上我们并夺取市场份额还需要一些时间。”云林工厂的具体产能、MOR与传统光刻胶的产出比例以及确切范围尚未披露。

history.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
history.description:
Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. 
Elon Musk, his financial manager and Neuralink CEO, Jared Birchall, and OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman have already testified before the jury. Shivon Zilis, a former OpenAI board member who shares four children with Musk, took the stand last week, and the courtroom also watched former OpenAI CTO Mura Murati’s videotaped deposition.
For the trial’s third week, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella appeared on Monday, followed by OpenAI cofounder and former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever. Altman took the stand on Tuesday to refute the Musk argument that he is a liar and a snake, while on the final day of testimony, Wednesday, we are hearing from Microsoft CTO Kevin Scott.
Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI says that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.
In his lawsuit, Musk is asking for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation. Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.
People to Know
Plaintiff
Elon Musk — plaintiff, OpenAI cofounder and now CEO of rival xAI
Steven Molo — lead counsel for the plaintiff
Jared Birchall — manager of Musk’s family office
Shivon Zilis — former OpenAI board member who shares multiple children with Musk
Defendant
Sam Altman — defendant, CEO of OpenAI 
William Savitt — lead counsel for the defendant
Greg Brockman — president of OpenAI as well as a cofounder 
Ilya Sutskever — former chief scientist at OpenAI and a cofounder
Judge
Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers — aka YGR, trial judge

Achiam is running circles around this lawyer on cross, without doing the annoying things other witnesses have done.
			
Okay, it’s time for the cross of Achiam.
			
“I think he was just upset that he had been challenged,” Achiam said. “This was not friendly.”

We are now looking at Helen Toner’s deposition.

Shivon Zilis brainstormed possible scenarios for AI.
			
Musk offered Sam Altman a board seat at Tesla…
			
Shivon’s emails aren’t great for Musk.

Also in the spirit of clarifications this morning…

When asked how much Musk works per week, Zilis laughed.
			
Musk’s team has called Shivon Zilis.

We are seeing video testimony from Mira Murati’s deposition.
			
We are clearing up “a few inaccuracies from yesterday.”
			
We are taking care of some matters before the jury comes in.

Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.People to KnowPlaintiffElon Musk — plaintiff, OpenAI cofounder and now CEO of rival xAISteven Molo — lead counsel for the plaintiffJared Birchall — manager of Musk’s family officeShivon Zilis — former OpenAI board member who shares multiple children with MuskDefendantSam Altman — defendant, CEO of OpenAIWilliam Savitt — lead counsel for the defendantGreg Brockman — president of OpenAI as well as a cofounderIlya Sutskever — former chief scientist at OpenAI and a cofounderJudgeYvonne Gonzalez Rogers — aka YGR, trial judgeHere’s all the latest on the trial between Musk and Altman:
history.author: Elizabeth Lopatto
history.titleZh: 埃隆·马斯克与山姆·阿尔特曼就OpenAI未来之争的法庭交锋实时更新
history.descriptionZh:
2024年，一场备受瞩目的高 stakes 诉讼正在美国法庭上演：特斯拉CEO埃隆·马斯克（Elon Musk）与OpenAI首席执行官山姆·奥特曼（Sam Altman）对簿公堂，这场审判可能重塑OpenAI及旗下明星产品ChatGPT的未来走向。马斯克作为OpenAI的联合创始人之一，于2024年提起诉讼，指控OpenAI背离了其创立时“开发AI以造福人类”的初衷，转而将重心放在追逐利润上。

本案的核心争议在于：马斯克声称奥特曼和另一位联合创始人格雷格·布罗克曼（Greg Brockman）诱骗他出资，随后却抛弃了最初的非营利性目标。而OpenAI方面则反驳称，“这起诉讼一直是毫无根据且出于嫉妒的企图，旨在拖垮竞争对手”，借此为马斯克自己的SpaceX、xAI和X等公司铺路——这些公司推出了AI聊天机器人Grok，与ChatGPT直接竞争。

审判已进入第三周，多位关键人物先后出庭作证。包括马斯克的财务主管兼Neuralink CEO贾里德·伯查尔（Jared Birchall）、OpenAI联合创始人布罗克曼、前OpenAI董事会成员兼马斯克四个孩子的母亲希冯·齐里斯（Shivon Zilis），以及前OpenAI CTO穆拉·穆拉蒂（Mura Murati）的视频证词。周一，微软CEO萨提亚·纳德拉（Satya Nadella）出庭；随后OpenAI联合创始人、前首席科学家伊利亚·苏茨克维尔（Ilya Sutskever）也站上了证人席。周二，奥特曼本人作证，反驳马斯克关于他是“骗子和蛇”的指控。周三，微软CTO凯文·斯科特（Kevin Scott）是最后一位作证的关键人物。

马斯克在诉讼中要求法庭罢免奥特曼和布罗克曼的职务，并禁止OpenAI以公共利益公司（public benefit corporation）的形式运营。此外，如果马斯克胜诉，他还要求OpenAI的非营利组织获得高达1500亿美元的赔偿金。

这场审判的法官是伊冯·冈萨雷斯·罗杰斯（Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers，简称YGR）。以下是本案的关键人物一览：

- **原告方**：埃隆·马斯克（原告，OpenAI联合创始人，现xAI CEO）；首席律师史蒂文·莫洛（Steven Molo）；证人贾里德·伯查尔（马斯克家族办公室管理者）、希冯·齐里斯（前OpenAI董事）。
- **被告方**：山姆·奥特曼（被告，OpenAI CEO）；首席律师威廉·萨维特（William Savitt）；格雷格·布罗克曼（OpenAI总裁兼联合创始人）；伊利亚·苏茨克维尔（前首席科学家、联合创始人）。
- **法官**：伊冯·冈萨雷斯·罗杰斯。

目前，审判仍在进行中，双方围绕OpenAI的使命转变、资金使用和领导层诚信展开激烈交锋。其结果不仅关乎OpenAI的治理结构，更可能为整个AI行业的非营利与营利之争树立判例。

history.title: AMD expands its Ryzen 9000 PRO lineup with six new SKUs, now featuring 3D V-Cache for the first time — new workstation CPUs have up to 170W TDPs, available with OEMs later this year
history.description: The Ryzen 9000 family is expanding with six new PRO series models that break two rules: higher than 65W TDPs and 3D V-Cache stacked underneath the CCDs. It's the first time we're seeing 120W to 170W SKUs in this lineup, along with the introduction of X3D models. General availability beings in Q3 2026.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter AMD has just launched six new Ryzen PRO series CPUs as part of its Ryzen 9000 family, bringing 3D V-Cache for the first time to this lineup, along with unlocking higher TDPs. The Ryzen PRO series is intended for workstation users who prioritize security and stability, even though they otherwise use identical silicon to mainstream desktop parts. The new CPUs range from 6 cores all the way up to 16 cores, so let's break them all down.At the very top, we have the flagship Ryzen 9 PRO 9965X3D rocking 16x Zen 5 cores that can boost up to 5.5 GHz. It features 128MB of total L3 cache — either 8-core CCD already has the standard 32MB of L3 cache, but one of the clusters gets another 64MB of X3D stacked underneath. The CPU has a 170W TDP, which is the same as the 9950X3D it's based on, marking the first time a Ryzen PRO CPU has broken the 65W power limit.Then there's the Ryzen 9 PRO 9965; basically the same CPU but just without the extra cache. It also features 16x Zen 5 cores with a 5.4 GHz boost clock and a 170W TDP. The L3 cache is limited to 64MB since it has no 3D V-Cache. Moreover, the Ryzen 7 PRO 9955 brings 12x Zen 5 cores with maximum speeds of 5.4 GHz and a 120W TDP, once again rocking just 64MB of L3 cache.Latest Videos From You may like AMD Ryzen 9 PRO 9965X3D spotted on Passmark AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 unveiled as first dual-cache X3D CPU AMD details Ryzen AI 400 desktop with up to 8 cores, Radeon 860M graphics Swipe to scroll horizontallyRyzen 9000 PRO Series (emboldened represents new SKUs)CPU Model CoresThreadsMax. Boost ClockBase ClockAMD Ryzen™ 9 PRO 9965X3D 1632Up to 5.5 GHz4.3 GHzAMD Ryzen™ 9 PRO 99651632Up to 5.5 GHz4.3 GHzAMD Ryzen™ 9 PRO 99551224Up to 5.4 GHz3.4 GHzAMD Ryzen™ 9 PRO 99451224Up to 5.4 GHz3.4 GHzAMD Ryzen™ 7 PRO 9755X3D816Up to 5.2 GHz4.7 GHzAMD Ryzen™ 7 PRO 9755816Up to 5.4 GHz3.8 GHzAMD Ryzen™ 7 PRO 9745816Up to 5.4 GHz3.8 GHzAMD Ryzen™ 5 PRO 9655612Up to 5.4 GHz3.9 GHzAMD Ryzen™ 5 PRO 9645612Up to 5.4 GHz3.9 GHzThe Ryzen 7 PRO 99755X3D is the other X3D-branded chip in this lineup, housing 8x Zen 5 cores that can clock up to 5.2 GHz on a TDP of 120W. This chip has a 92MB L3 cache pool since there's only one 8-core CCD to stack the 64MB of 3D V-Cache under. You can also get the same CPU with just 32MB of L3 in the form of the Ryzen 7 PRO 9755, featuring otherwise identical specs to its X3D counterpart.Lastly, the Ryzen 5 PRO 9655 enters the battle as a true midrange offering, sporting 6x Zen 5 cores with a 5.4 GHz boost clock but still a 120W TDP. That makes it quite unique from the other 6-core offering, the Ryzen 5 PRO 96455, which is limited to just 65W. For a while, all Ryzen PRO SKUs were restricted to that number to maximize efficiency, but it looks like AMD is changing gears to allow even relatively budget parts to consume more power. The Ryzen 5 Pro 9655 has 32MB of L3 cache as well.That wraps up all the new SKUs, expanding the Ryzen 9000 PRO family to accommodate every tier of managed or workstation computing. Now, keep in mind that these CPUs aren't available for sale via regular retail channels. Instead, you'll find these at OEMs, such as Lenovo, which is already working to launch its ThinkStation P4 in Q3 2026. That's about all the details we have on availability, while pricing is never disclosed formally for PRO series SKUs anyway. history.author: Hassam Nasir
history.titleZh: AMD扩展锐龙9000 PRO系列，新增六款型号，首次搭载3D V-Cache——新款工作站CPU最高TDP达170W，将于今年晚些时候向OEM供货
history.descriptionZh:
AMD近日正式发布六款全新Ryzen PRO 9000系列处理器，首次为工作站级PRO产品线引入3D V-Cache技术，并解锁更高热设计功耗（TDP）。这批芯片面向注重安全性与稳定性的企业用户，虽然核心硅片与主流桌面版完全相同，但通过严格的验证和固件优化满足商用场景需求。新系列覆盖6核心到16核心，下面逐一解析技术细节。

**旗舰型号：Ryzen 9 PRO 9965X3D**  
这是PRO系列首款搭载3D V-Cache的顶级型号，采用16个Zen 5核心（32线程），最高加速频率5.5 GHz，基础频率4.3 GHz。其L3缓存总计128 MB：两个8核CCD各自配备标准的32 MB L3，其中一组CCD下方额外堆叠了64 MB 3D V-Cache（通过硅穿孔垂直互联）。TDP达到170 W，与桌面版Ryzen 9 9950X3D一致，标志着PRO系列终于突破此前长期保持的65 W功耗限制。这意味着在长时间高负载任务中，它可以持续释放更强性能，不再受限于保守的功耗墙。

**同门无X3D版：Ryzen 9 PRO 9965**  
规格几乎相同：16核32线程，最高加速5.5 GHz，TDP同为170 W。但缺少3D V-Cache，因此L3缓存总量降至64 MB（每个CCD 32 MB）。适合对缓存容量不敏感、更依赖多核吞吐的计算任务。

**12核中坚：Ryzen 7 PRO 9955**  
搭载12个Zen 5核心（24线程），最高加速5.4 GHz，TDP 120 W，L3缓存64 MB。与16核型号相比，核心数减少但频率依然较高，可视为平衡核心数与功耗的定位。

**X3D次旗舰：Ryzen 7 PRO 9755X3D**  
这是第二款X3D型号，采用单CCD设计（8核16线程），最高加速5.2 GHz，TDP 120 W。由于只有一个CCD，额外堆叠的64 MB 3D V-Cache加上原本32 MB，使L3总容量达到92 MB。适合游戏、数据库查询、金融模拟等对缓存延迟高度敏感的应用。同时提供无X3D版本Ryzen 7 PRO 9755，核心数、频率和TDP完全相同，但L3仅32 MB，适合成本敏感或对缓存不敏感的负载。

**6核心入门：Ryzen 5 PRO 9655与9645**  
Ryzen 5 PRO 9655拥有6核心12线程，最高加速5.4 GHz，TDP却达到120 W，这与以往PRO系列低端型号仅65 W的做法截然不同。它配备32 MB L3缓存。而另一款Ryzen 5 PRO 96455则维持65 W TDP，频率和核心规格相同（5.4 GHz加速？据表格为5.4 GHz但基频可能不同），面向追求能效比的场景。AMD此次放宽功耗限制，即使是中低端型号也允许更高功耗，从而获得更高全核加速，摆脱了以往仅靠能效打天下的策略。

**技术原理与创新点**  
3D V-Cache是AMD的缓存堆叠技术，通过在CCD上垂直集成额外64 MB SRAM，并通过硅通孔（TSV）和混合键合实现与计算核心的高速互联，延迟接近片上缓存。相比增大L3面积，堆叠方案不增加芯片平面面积，可在相同封装内缓存容量翻倍，显著降低缓存未命中率。对于数据库、科学计算、内容创作等访问模式不规则的应用，性能提升可达两位数百分比。此次将其下放到PRO系列，意味着企业级工作站也能享受这一红利。

**性能参数对比**  
与上一代Ryzen PRO 7000系列相比（TDP最高65W），新一代9000系列功耗大幅跃升：旗舰从65W到170W，增幅162%；中端从65W到120W；甚至入门6核也提供120W选项。缓存方面，16核X3D版本达到128MB（上一代最高仅64MB），8核X3D版本92MB。频率方面，16核加速5.5GHz，12核5.4GHz，8核5.2-5.4GHz，6核5.4GHz，均高于上代对应型号。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
这些处理器将直接嵌入OEM厂商的商用工作站和高端桌面机中，例如联想已宣布将在2026年第三季度推出搭载该系列的ThinkStation P4工作站。由于PRO系列不通过零售渠道销售，企业用户只能通过戴尔、惠普、联想等厂商的整机采购。应用场景涵盖：金融高频交易（低延迟缓存）、3D建模与渲染（多核高功耗释放）、AI推理（大缓存提升模型加载效率）、虚拟化与高密度计算（安全性与稳定性优先）。解锁TDP后，这些芯片在长时间满载下的性能表现将大幅优于以往PRO产品，缩小了与桌面旗舰的差距。AMD这一举动表明，工作站市场对绝对性能的需求正在超过对极致能效的追求，也为后续提升与Intel Xeon W系列的竞争筹码奠定了基础。

（以上摘要基于Tom's Hardware报道，具体型号以官方发布为准。）

history.title: NVIDIA, Ineffable Intelligence Team Up to Build the Future of Reinforcement Learning Infrastructure
history.description: Reinforcement-learning agents — AI systems that learn by trial and error — can convert computation into new knowledge. That’s the focus of a new engineering-level collaboration between NVIDIA and Ineffable Intelligence, the London-based AI lab founded by AlphaGo architect David Silver in the wake of Ineffable’s emergence from stealth last week. “The next frontier of […]
history.content: Reinforcement-learning agents — AI systems that learn by trial and error — can convert computation into new knowledge. That’s the focus of a new engineering-level collaboration between NVIDIA and Ineffable Intelligence, the London-based AI lab founded by AlphaGo architect David Silver in the wake of Ineffable’s emergence from stealth last week. “The next frontier of AI is superlearners — systems that learn continuously from experience,” said Jensen Huang, founder and CEO of NVIDIA. “We are thrilled to partner with Ineffable Intelligence to codesign the infrastructure for large-scale reinforcement learning as they push the frontier of AI and pioneer a new generation of intelligent systems.” Silver is one of the pioneers of reinforcement learning, an approach that has transformed AI research. He’s focused on further developing this approach into a new paradigm. “Researchers have largely solved the easier problem of AI: how to build systems that know all the things humans already know,” Silver said. “But now we need to solve the harder problem of AI: how to build systems that discover new knowledge for themselves. That requires a very different approach — systems that learn from experience.” That kind of learning needs a powerful and highly optimized pipeline to support it. Unlike pretraining, where a fixed dataset of human data flows through the system, reinforcement learning workloads generate their data on the fly. The system has to act, observe, score and update continuously in tight loops, which puts pressure on interconnect, memory bandwidth and serving in ways that pretraining doesn’t. Furthermore, the system will train on rich forms of experience that are quite distinct from human language and other human data, and may require novel model architectures and training algorithms. That’s where NVIDIA and Ineffable are focusing their technical work: building a pipeline that can feed reinforcement learning systems at scale. Engineers from both companies have teamed up to explore the best way to create this training pipeline. This work is starting on NVIDIA Grace Blackwell, and will be among the first to explore the upcoming NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform. The goal is to understand the next generation of hardware and software that will be required as the AI world shifts beyond human data toward models that learn through simulation and experience. Getting this infrastructure right will unlock an unprecedented scale of reinforcement learning in highly complex and rich environments, allowing agents to discover breakthroughs across all fields of knowledge.
history.author: NVIDIA Writers
history.titleZh: 英伟达与Ineffable Intelligence团队携手共建强化学习基础设施的未来
history.descriptionZh:
英伟达（NVIDIA）与伦敦AI实验室Ineffable Intelligence宣布达成一项尖端工程合作，旨在为大规模强化学习（Reinforcement Learning）构建全新的基础设施。Ineffable Intelligence的创始人是AlphaGo的核心架构师David Silver，该实验室刚于上周结束隐身模式正式亮相。此次合作标志着AI从“人类知识复现”向“自主经验学习”的关键转型。

**背景与上下文**  
传统AI系统大多依赖固定的人类标注数据进行预训练，本质上是“让机器学会人类已知的一切”。然而，David Silver指出，AI真正的下一道难题是构建能够自主发现新知识的系统，而非仅仅模仿。强化学习Agent通过与环境交互试错，将计算转化为新知识，这正是“超级学习者”（Superlearners）概念的核心——系统持续从经验中学习，而非静态数据集。英伟达创始人兼CEO黄仁勋表示，与Ineffable的合作旨在共同设计大规模强化学习的基础设施，推动AI前沿突破。

**核心技术原理与创新点**  
与传统预训练不同，强化学习的工作负载具有动态生成数据的本质特征：系统必须实时执行“动作-观测-打分-更新”的紧密循环。这种连续交互对以下三个方面提出了极高要求：  
- **互连带宽**：Agent与模拟环境之间的高频数据交换需要极低延迟的通信。  
- **内存带宽**：实时存储和访问大量经验轨迹（experience trajectories）以更新模型权重。  
- **服务框架**：在训练过程中同步部署策略推理和值函数评估，要求推理与训练深度耦合。

这些压力在预训练中几乎不存在，因为预训练数据是静态、预先加载的。此外，强化学习训练所依赖的“经验”并非人类语言或图像，而是复杂模拟环境中的多维信号（如物理交互、博弈状态、连续控制变量），这可能催生全新的模型架构和训练算法。因此，双方合作的重点是设计一条能够大规模“喂养”强化学习系统的流水线（pipeline），支持从模拟中实时生成并消化数据。

**性能参数与平台落地**  
该合作始于英伟达Grace Blackwell平台（结合Grace CPU和Blackwell GPU的高能效超级芯片），并将首批探索即将推出的Vera Rubin平台（英伟达下一代数据中心架构）。Grace Blackwell已在带宽和内存一致性方面针对AI工作负载优化，而Vera Rubin专为“数据流型”计算设计，其海量互连带宽和异构内存池正是为强化学习这种动态数据生成场景量身打造。工程师们将共同评估不同配置下的吞吐量、训练步数/秒、以及经验池的访问效率等关键指标，目标是构建一套能够支持数万亿交互步数的训练系统。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
若成功，这套基础设施将解锁前所未有的强化学习规模，使Agent能够在极其复杂和丰富的模拟环境中运行（如机器人全身控制、自动驾驶多智能体博弈、材料设计分子动力模拟、经济学策略推演等）。这些环境需具备高保真物理引擎和无限变化的可能性，从而让Agent自主发现科学、工程和商业领域的新突破——这超越了人类专家能给出的任何数据集。例如，在药物研发中，强化学习Agent可自行探索数百万种化学反应路径；在气候模型中，它能寻找最优减排策略；在游戏和机器人领域，则能实现从零样本到大师级技能的自我进化。总之，这一合作将推动AI从“模仿已知”迈向“发现未知”，而英伟达的硬件-软件协同设计正是支撑这一跃迁的基石。

history.title: Hermes Unlocks Self-Improving AI Agents, Powered by NVIDIA RTX PCs and DGX Spark
history.description: Agentic AI is changing the way users get work done. Following the success of OpenClaw, the community is embracing new open source agentic frameworks. The latest is Hermes Agent, which crossed 140,000 GitHub stars in under three months.
history.content: Agentic AI is changing the way users get work done. Following the success of OpenClaw, the community is embracing new open source agentic frameworks. The latest is Hermes Agent, which crossed 140,000 GitHub stars in under three months and, as of last week, is the most used agent in the world according to OpenRouter. Developed by Nous Research, Hermes is designed for reliability and self-improvement — two qualities that have historically been hard to achieve with agents. It’s provider- and model-agnostic by design, and optimized for always-on local use, making NVIDIA RTX PCs, NVIDIA RTX PRO workstations and NVIDIA DGX Spark the ideal hardware to run it at full speed, around the clock. Qwen 3.6, a new series of high-performance, open weight large language models (LLMs) from Alibaba, are ideal for running local agents like Hermes. The Qwen 3.6 27B and 35B parameter models are outperforming their previous-generation 120B and 400B parameter model counterparts and run on NVIDIA RTX and DGX Spark for accelerated agentic AI. Hermes: Local AI Agent Capabilities Accelerated Like other popular agents, Hermes integrates with messaging apps, can access local files and applications, and runs 24/7. But four standout capabilities set it apart: Self-Evolving Skills: Hermes writes and refines its own skills. Every time the agent encounters a complex task or receives feedback, it saves its learnings as a skill so it can adapt and improve over time. Contained Sub-Agents: Hermes treats sub-agents as short-lived, isolated workers dedicated to a sub-task — with a focused context and set of tools. This keeps task organization tidy, minimizes confusion for the agent and allows Hermes to run with smaller context windows, which is ideal for local models. Reliability by design: Nous Research curates and stress-tests every skill, tool and plug-in that ships with Hermes. The result: Hermes just works — even with 30 billion-parameter-class local models — without the constant debugging that most other agent frameworks require. Same model, better results: Developer comparisons using identical models across frameworks consistently show stronger results in Hermes. The difference is the framework: Hermes is an active orchestration layer, not a thin wrapper, enabling persistent, on-device agents instead of task-by-task execution. Both the Hermes agent and the underlying LLM are built to run locally — which means the quality of hardware directly determines the quality of a user’s experience. NVIDIA RTX GPUs are purpose-built for this kind of workload. Qwen 3.6: Data Center-Level Intelligence, Locally The latest Qwen 3.6 models build on the acclaimed Qwen 3.5 series to deliver another leap forward for local AI agents. The new Qwen 3.6 35B model runs on roughly 20GB of memory while surpassing 120 billion-parameter models, which require 70GB+ of memory. In addition, Qwen 3.6 27B is a new, dense model with more active parameters — matching the accuracy of 400 billion-parameter models like Qwen 3.5 397B while being one-sixteenth the size. Running on high-end RTX GPUs provides the model the computing power it needs for a speedy experience. These models are ideal for local agents like Hermes, and NVIDIA GPUs and DGX Spark are the fastest way to run them. NVIDIA Tensor Cores accelerate AI inference to deliver higher throughput and lower latency — so Hermes can work through a multistep task or refine one of its own skills in seconds rather than minutes. DGX Spark: The Always-On Agentic Computer Agents like Hermes are built to run continuously — responding to requests, planning multistep tasks, executing autonomously and self-improving. NVIDIA DGX Spark is the ideal companion — a compact, efficient standalone machine built for sustained, all-day agentic workflows. With 128GB of unified memory and 1 petaflop of AI performance, NVIDIA DGX Spark can run 120 billion-parameter mixture-of-experts models all day. And the new Qwen 3.6 35B model delivers equivalent intelligence in a leaner footprint — running faster and giving users the capacity to run concurrent workloads. To maximize performance and ease of use, read the Hermes DGX Spark playbook. Plus, register for upcoming hands-on sessions in NVIDIA’s “Build It Yourself” agentic AI series to learn how to build autonomous AI agents with NemoClaw and OpenShell. NVIDIA DGX Spark is available to order from NVIDIA’s manufacturing partners — visit the marketplace. Getting Started With Hermes on NVIDIA Hardware Running Hermes locally on NVIDIA hardware is straightforward. Visit the Hermes GitHub repository to get started, and pair it with a preferred local model and runtime. Run Hermes alongside Qwen 3.6 via llama.cpp, LM Studio or Ollama. Hermes Agent ships with LM Studio and Ollama support out of the box for the simplest path to a local agent. Whether for a local AI enthusiast exploring the frontier of personal agents or a developer building local tooling for their workflows, Hermes on NVIDIA hardware offers a uniquely capable and reliable foundation. Stay tuned for more updates from RTX AI Garage on the latest open models and agents optimized for NVIDIA RTX hardware. #ICYMI: The Latest From RTX AI Garage ✨ NVIDIA RTX PRO GPUs deliver up to 3x faster token generation running Qwen 3.6 models with llama.cpp. Get the real-time responsiveness needed for local AI, where agents can tackle multistep tasks and refine their skills to keep workflows seamless. Google’s Gemma 4 26B and 31B models now available as NVFP4 checkpoints for even faster performance on NVIDIA Blackwell GPUs. Pair the NVFP4 checkpoints with Google’s new Multi-Token Prediction drafters to get up to 3x faster inference at identical output quality, enabling frontier-class reasoning to run locally on NVIDIA GPUs. Mistral Medium version 3.5, also released in April, includes compatibility updates with llama.cpp and Ollama, enabling users to run on NVIDIA RTX PRO and DGX Spark systems. 🦞 NVIDIA recently introduced NVIDIA NemoClaw, an open source stack that optimizes OpenClaw experiences on NVIDIA devices by increasing security and supporting local models. NemoClaw now supports Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL2), bringing support to enthusiasts and developers on Microsoft’s platform. Get started with NemoClaw on DGX Spark with this step-by-step playbook. Plug in to NVIDIA AI PC on Facebook, Instagram, TikTok and X — and stay informed by subscribing to the RTX AI PC newsletter. Follow NVIDIA Workstation on LinkedIn and X. See notice regarding software product information.
history.author: Abhishek Gore
history.titleZh: Hermes Unlocks Self-Improving AI Agents, Powered by NVIDIA RTX PCs and DGX Spark
history.descriptionZh:
Agentic AI 正在重塑用户完成工作的方式。继 OpenClaw 成功之后，社区积极拥抱新的开源代理框架。最新代表是 Hermes Agent，它在不到三个月内获得超过 14 万 GitHub 星标，并根据 OpenRouter 数据，上周已成为全球使用率最高的智能体。该框架由 Nous Research 开发，专为可靠性和自我改进而设计——这两项能力过去在代理中难以实现。Hermes 天然与提供商和模型无关，并针对始终在线的本地使用进行了优化，因此 NVIDIA RTX PC、NVIDIA RTX PRO 工作站和 NVIDIA DGX Spark 成为其全速、全天候运行的理想硬件。阿里巴巴推出的全新高性能开源权重大语言模型系列 Qwen 3.6，是运行 Hermes 这类本地代理的理想选择。Qwen 3.6 的 27B 和 35B 参数模型，性能超越其上一代 120B 和 400B 参数模型，并可在 NVIDIA RTX 和 DGX Spark 上加速运行。

### Hermes：加速本地 AI 代理能力

与其他流行代理类似，Hermes 可集成消息应用、访问本地文件和应用程序，并 7×24 小时运行。但其四项突出能力使其与众不同：

- **自进化技能**：Hermes 能自行编写并优化自己的技能。每当代理遇到复杂任务或收到反馈时，它会将学到的经验保存为技能，从而不断适应和改进。
- **包含性子代理**：Hermes 将子代理视为短暂、隔离的工作者，专门负责子任务——拥有独立的上下文和工具集。这保持了任务组织的清晰，最大限度减少代理的混乱，并允许 Hermes 在更小的上下文窗口中运行，非常适合本地模型。
- **设计上的可靠性**：Nous Research 对随 Hermes 发布的每一技能、工具和插件都进行策划和压力测试。结果是：即使使用 30B 参数级别的本地模型，Hermes 也能稳定运行，无需像大多数其他代理框架那样持续调试。
- **相同模型，更优结果**：开发者使用相同模型在不同框架间对比，一致显示 Hermes 能获得更强结果。差异在于框架本身：Hermes 是一个主动编排层，而非薄薄的封装，从而实现持久的设备端代理，而非逐任务执行。

Hermes 代理及其底层 LLM 都为本地运行而构建——这意味着硬件质量直接决定用户体验。NVIDIA RTX GPU 为这类工作负载量身定制。

### Qwen 3.6：数据中心级智能，本地运行

最新的 Qwen 3.6 模型建立在广受好评的 Qwen 3.5 系列之上，为本地 AI 代理带来又一次飞跃。新推出的 Qwen 3.6 35B 模型仅需约 20GB 内存运行，却超越了需要 70GB+ 内存的 120B 参数模型。此外，Qwen 3.6 27B 是一款全新的密集模型，拥有更多活跃参数——其精度与 Qwen 3.5 397B 等 400B 参数模型相当，但尺寸仅为后者的十六分之一。在高端 RTX GPU 上运行时，模型获得快速体验所需的计算能力。这些模型非常适合 Hermes 这样的本地代理，而 NVIDIA GPU 和 DGX Spark 是运行它们的最快途径。NVIDIA Tensor Core 加速 AI 推理，实现更高吞吐量和更低延迟——使得 Hermes 可以在数秒内完成多步骤任务或优化自身技能，而无需数分钟。

### DGX Spark：始终在线的代理计算机

Hermes 这类代理被设计为持续运行——响应请求、规划多步骤任务、自主执行并自我改进。NVIDIA DGX Spark 是理想的伴侣——它是一款紧凑、高效的独立机器，专为全天候代理工作流而构建。凭借 128GB 统一内存和 1 petaflop 的 AI 性能，NVIDIA DGX Spark 可以全天运行 120B 参数的混合专家模型。而新的 Qwen 3.6 35B 模型以更精简的足迹提供了同等智能——运行更快，并为用户提供了运行并发工作负载的余地。为最大化性能和易用性，可阅读 Hermes DGX Spark 操作指南。此外，可注册参加 NVIDIA“Build It Yourself”代理 AI 系列即将举办的动手实践课程，学习如何使用 NemoClaw 和 OpenShell 构建自主 AI 代理。NVIDIA DGX Spark 可通过 NVIDIA 制造合作伙伴订购——请访问市场页面。

### 在 NVIDIA 硬件上开始使用 Hermes

在 NVIDIA 硬件上本地运行 Hermes 非常简单。访问 Hermes GitHub 仓库开始，并将其与首选本地模型和运行时配对。通过 llama.cpp、LM Studio 或 Ollama 运行 Hermes 与 Qwen 3.6。Hermes Agent 出厂即支持 LM Studio 和 Ollama，是通往本地代理的最简路径。无论是探索个人代理前沿的本地 AI 爱好者，还是为工作流构建本地工具的开发者，NVIDIA 硬件上的 Hermes 都提供了独特、可靠且强大的基础。敬请关注 RTX AI Garage 关于为 NVIDIA RTX 硬件优化的最新开放模型和代理的更多更新。

#ICYMI：RTX AI Garage 最新动态
- NVIDIA RTX PRO GPU 在使用 llama.cpp 运行 Qwen 3.6 模型时，token 生成速度提升高达 3 倍。为本地 AI 提供所需的实时响应能力，使代理能够处理多步骤任务并优化技能以保持工作流无缝。
- Google 的 Gemma 4 26B 和 31B 模型现已提供 NVFP4 检查点，可在 NVIDIA Blackwell GPU 上实现更快性能。将 NVFP4 检查点与 Google 新的多 Token 预测草稿器配对，可在相同输出质量下获得高达 3 倍推理加速，使前沿推理能力可在 NVIDIA GPU 上本地运行。
- Mistral Medium 版本 3.5（亦于四月发布）包含与 llama.cpp 和 Ollama 的兼容性更新，使用户能在 NVIDIA RTX PRO 和 DGX Spark 系统上运行。
- 🦞 NVIDIA 近期推出了 NVIDIA NemoClaw，这是一个开源堆栈，通过增强安全性和支持本地模型，优化了 NVIDIA 设备上的 OpenClaw 体验。NemoClaw 现已支持 Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL2)，将支持带到微软平台的爱好者和开发者面前。按照这份逐步操作指南在 DGX Spark 上开始使用 NemoClaw。

请关注 NVIDIA AI PC 的 Facebook、Instagram、TikTok 和 X 账号，并订阅 RTX AI PC 新闻邮件以获取最新信息。在 LinkedIn 和 X 上关注 NVIDIA Workstation。请注意关于软件产品信息的声明。

history.title: Intel, Qualcomm confirm Googlebook AI laptop partnerships, opening Arm and x86 possibilities for new OS — Google VP says devices to also ship with MediaTek chips
history.description: Intel has officially confirmed its partnership with Googlebook as Google prepares a new lineup of Gemini-powered AI laptops.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 4 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Chipmakers are taking to social media to confirm their partnerships with Google on its newly announced Googlebook laptop lineup. In a post shared on X, Intel said it is collaborating on the lineup. Meanwhile, over on Instagram, Qualcomm made its own confirmation. Both used similar wording, saying that the laptops will be "powerful" and "premium" "devices designed for Intelligence." (Qualcomm used "built" instead of designed."The announcements came shortly after Google gave a preview of its upcoming platform at the Android Show: I/O Edition, and confirmed that it is working with various PC manufacturers, including HP, Dell, Acer, Asus, and Lenovo.During the showcase, Google refrained from discussing the core hardware and instead focused entirely on its brand-new operating system, which combines elements of Android and ChromeOS with deep Gemini Intelligence integration. It was initially assumed that the new Googlebook lineup would be based on Arm SoCs, since many aspects of the platform resemble an Android smartphone or tablet experience. However, with Intel now officially involved, there is a possibility that Google’s new AI-focused OS could also support x86 hardware, unless Intel has an Arm-based chip up its sleeve.In an exclusive interview with Chrome Unboxed, Google VP John Maletis further confirmed Intel’s involvement in the Googlebook project, revealing that the upcoming notebooks will ship with processors from Intel, Qualcomm, and MediaTek. According to Maletis, the Googlebook is an entirely new category of premium AI-first laptops that deeply integrate Gemini into the core experience rather than treating AI as an add-on. He also noted that Google is establishing strict hardware standards across memory, storage, keyboards, and overall build quality to ensure every Googlebook delivers a consistent premium experience.Latest Videos From You may like Google's new laptop platform, 'Googlebook' leaks ahead of reveal event Intel and Google announce multi-year chip deal Nvidia's Chinese competitor Moore Threads launches new "MTT AI Book" laptop featuring custom 12-core Arm chip The interview also shed more light on what users can expect when Googlebook devices officially launch later this fall. According to Maletis, the first wave of laptops will focus heavily on premium hardware from its partners, while also bringing back the iconic Glow Bar LED lighting seen on older Chromebook Pixel devices. He additionally confirmed that Googlebook laptops will run native Android applications without emulation, promising significantly better app performance alongside tighter Android smartphone integration and Gemini-powered features such as the new Magic Pointer interface.Interestingly, the Googlebook partnership comes just a month after Intel and Google announced a separate multi-year agreement focused on next-generation AI cloud infrastructure. Under the deal, Google Cloud will deploy Intel Xeon processors alongside custom IPUs for large-scale AI workloads, suggesting that the relationship between the two companies now extends from cloud AI infrastructure all the way down to consumer AI-focused devices.Updated May 13, 3:18 PM ET with further confirmation from Qualcomm on its partnership with Google Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
history.author: Kunal Khullar
history.titleZh: 英特尔和高通确认与Googlebook AI笔记本电脑的合作，为新操作系统开辟Arm和x86的可能性——谷歌副总裁称设备也将搭载联发科芯片
history.descriptionZh:
Google 最新发布的 Googlebook 笔记本电脑系列引发了芯片厂商的密集关注。英特尔在 X 平台、高通在 Instagram 上相继发文确认合作，均用“强大”“高端”“为智能设计”等措辞形容该系列产品。这一动向紧随 Google 在 Android Show: I/O Edition 上对平台的预览，当时 Google 明确表示正与惠普、戴尔、宏碁、华硕和联想等 PC 制造商合作。

在展示中，Google 并未透露核心硬件细节，而是全力聚焦全新操作系统——该系统融合了 Android 和 ChromeOS 的元素，并深度集成 Gemini 人工智能。起初业内普遍猜测 Googlebook 将基于 Arm 架构 SoC，因为平台体验与 Android 智能手机或平板十分相似。但随着英特尔的官方参与，情况出现变化：除非英特尔拥有 Arm 芯片，否则这意味着 Google 全新的 AI 操作系统可能同时支持 x86 硬件。

通过与 Chrome Unboxed 的独家对话，Google 副总裁 John Maletis 进一步印证了英特尔的参与，并透露 Googlebook 将搭载来自英特尔、高通和联发科的处理器。Maletis 强调，Googlebook 是一个全新的“AI 优先”高端笔记本电脑品类，Gemini 被深度集成到核心体验中，而非作为附加功能。Google 还为内存、存储、键盘及整体做工质量制定了严格的硬件标准，以确保每一台 Googlebook 都能提供一致的高端体验。

关于上市细节，Maletis 表示首批 Googlebook 设备将于今年秋季正式发布，重点聚焦合作伙伴的高端硬件，同时将带回老款 Chromebook Pixel 上标志性的 Glow Bar LED 灯带设计。他还确认，Googlebook 笔记本将原生运行 Android 应用程序，无需模拟，从而显著提升应用性能，并与 Android 智能手机实现更紧密的集成。Gemini 驱动的功能如新的 Magic Pointer 界面也将一并搭载。

值得注意的是，这一合作仅在上月英特尔与 Google 宣布一项为期多年的 AI 云基础设施协议之后。根据该协议，Google Cloud 将部署英特尔至强处理器以及定制 IPU，用于大规模 AI 工作负载。这表明两家公司的合作关系已从云端 AI 基础设施延伸至消费级 AI 设备端。

history.title: Data centers are coming for rural America
history.description:

history.content: At its peak, the Androscoggin paper mill in Jay, Maine, a rural town about 67 miles northwest of Portland, employed about 1,500 people — until a pulp digester exploded in 2020, forcing the mill to close permanently.In 2023, the 1.4 million-square-foot facility was purchased through a joint venture by JGT2 Redevelopment and a number of other holding and capital companies. The project is led by developer Tony McDonald. Over the next three years, McDonald and his team broke down the mill’s machinery and shipped it to Pakistan, and worked to clean up the industrial site for resale. That resale agreement was finalized earlier this year, according to McDonald — turning Jay into the latest flashpoint over giant data centers in America.Maine is particularly appealing for data center developers for its relatively cool year-round temperatures, lax land-use statutes, and 54 percent renewable energy mix, the eighth highest in the nation. There is a handful of planned data centers around the state, which recently prompted the state legislature to pass a bill ordering an 18-month moratorium on permits and building of any proposed data center that consumes more than 20 megawatts of power. Lawmakers wanted to pause construction in order to study data centers’ impact on local economies, the power grid, and the environment.But that bill, which would have been the country’s first, was vetoed by Maine Gov. Janet Mills last month. In her veto, she cited one overriding reason: jobs. A $550 million facility proposed for the shuttered paper mill in Jay, she argued, would create 125 to 150 permanent, high-paying positions in a town that had watched its largest employer close.From mill towns in Maine to farm counties in Indiana to desert plots outside Abilene, Texas, data center developers are telling local governments: Bring us in, give us what we need, add some tax breaks, and the jobs will follow. More than 35 states have responded by offering incentives and more to attract the industry.There’s little research into whether massive industrial sites actually deliver the long-term economic gains they promise, but early reports suggest otherwise. Experts say that rural communities often lack the governmental expertise to properly assess how data centers might impact an area. According to recent Pew Research Center data, 67 percent of planned data centers in the US are headed to rural areas, and 39 percent are going to counties that currently have none. As data center development scales rapidly, it’s becoming clear that what rural communities around the country are actually getting isn’t jobs, but a power- and water-hungry industrial facility that temporarily employs about as many people as a midsize restaurant.The Androscoggin Mill in Jay, Maine, closed in 2023 after an explosion. The site is now being considered for a data center. Image: The Boulos CompanyThe data center fight in MaineOriginally, Tony McDonald had planned to sell the mill to an oriented strand board company called Godfrey Forest Products, which would have employed approximately 150 people, he said. When federal tariffs killed the financial backing for that project, McDonald pivoted to an idea he’d been getting pitches about.“Most of the people that were contacting us, you know, they were all hat and no cattle,” McDonald said. He fielded multiple calls from what he terms “data center cowboys” who claimed to have one of the seven big tech companies as a client and were looking for a place to build a new data center. When he’d dig deeper on the caller, he’d find that they didn’t actually have the backing they claimed.After a few conversations, he began pursuing a data center partnership with Sentinel Data Centers, a New York-based company that specializes in data centers serving the healthcare, financial, and hyperscale industries, according to its website. Sentinel did not respond to multiple requests for comment for this story.McDonald says that, as he understands it, the project in Jay will be a neocloud data center, a specialized facility built to deliver high-performance GPU computing for AI and machine learning workloads. If a neocloud data center is going into the old mill, it will require more than 100kW of energy per rack, according to industry standards, and will need either direct-to-chip or immersion cooling, both of which require ample space and water resources.“Most of the people that were contacting us, you know, they were all hat and no cattle.”— Tony McDonald, JGT2 RedevelopmentWhile McDonald has repeatedly said that he is not interested in tax breaks for the project, he did want to leave the door open for potential tax benefits down the road if the town determines that it’s worth it. Just before Mills vetoed the moratorium, the Maine state legislature passed a law that excludes data centers from some of Maine’s tax breaks for businesses, but it leaves the door open for local municipalities to offer tax break agreements and other municipal incentives. That could spell trouble for small communities like Jay, hoping for a tax windfall to help them invest in schools, community buildings, and roadworks.Maine state Rep. Melanie Sachs, the sponsor of the Maine moratorium bill, claims that McDonald did not inform the Jay Select Board of the new plan to turn the mill into a data center until late February 2026, just days before her moratorium bill was scheduled for a floor vote. Her bill was first introduced to committee on January 30th.The Select Board heard McDonald’s presentation in March and voted 4-0 in support, according to The Maine Monitor. The moratorium bill passed both the state House of Representatives and Senate on April 14th, and Mills vetoed it on April 24th, citing the job creation in Jay as her reason.Sachs, who chairs Maine’s House Energy, Utilities and Technology Committee, said her legislation was never about banning data centers. “This bill was about creating the playbook,” she said. “And we were told, ‘Don’t worry your pretty little heads about it, data centers are not coming to Maine anytime soon.’ They came anyway, and without a framework in place, towns have no mechanism to evaluate the claims developers are making.”“Even if it’s 30 jobs, that means a lot to Jay, then, okay, but you’ve swept away protections for 1.4 million Mainers for 30 jobs,” Sachs said.The economics of data centersMichael Hicks, the director of the Center for Business and Economic Research at Ball State University, is an economist and a professor who last November published one of the first causal analyses of data center employment effects in the United States. He studied data center openings across 254 Texas counties and measured their actual effect on local long-term employment.He found that net job creation was effectively zero. Whatever long-term jobs existed at data centers were being offset by losses elsewhere in the same sector.”As you drive by a data center, you see people working on it,” Hicks said. “You see construction workers. The hotels locally are packed. But there’s no net pulse of that. A lot of these workers are there for three weeks to do their part of it, and then they’re gone … The real question is whether there are permanent jobs associated with data centers, and in Texas, the answer is no.”“A lot of these workers are there for three weeks to do their part of it, and then they’re gone.”— Michael Hicks, Ball State UniversityTexas serves as an ideal test bed because of its isolated grid and a mix of large, fast-growing metros and tech hubs alongside rural, remote towns, which mirrors the rest of the country. As Hicks puts it, it’s essentially a mini-United States with its variety of regions, and the economic lessons learned in Texas can be widely applied across the country.Rural towns are often “outgunned” when trying to negotiate deals with large data center builders, said Anthony Elmo, public education funding defender at Good Jobs First, a nonprofit research center focused on corporate and government accountability in economic development.“They don’t have the resources to negotiate,” said Elmo. “They don’t know what to ask for. They don’t have the legal expertise, and they don’t feel like they have the leverage, which I think is part of the issue.”On the national level, the math isn’t much better, either. Business Insider recently reported that the national subsidy for data centers exceeded $2 million in costs per permanent job, and in some cases, like one in New York, companies received nearly $77 million in tax breaks for a facility that created exactly one permanent position.Microsoft’s Quincy, Washington, facility, which is roughly comparable to the initial plan in Jay, employed as many as 500 workers during construction but now operates with just 50 full-time employees. The type of data center being built determines where on that spectrum of long-term employment a community lands. Neocloud data centers, like the one coming to Jay, can require 30 to 50 full-time staff, depending on size.“Of those, say, 50 jobs, a chunk of them are maintenance, a chunk of them are technicians in charge of backup generators. The high-tech jobs make up maybe 10 percent of the facility,” Elmo points out. And many times, data center companies will count remote workers in other states as employees of the state in which the data center is located. “We may get a little bit of an economic effect from that, but it isn’t nearly as much as if it were a physical person in Maine buying goods and adding to the local economy,” he said.What about upskilling?Most of the lobbying around data centers focuses on job creation and “upskilling,” or training workers for new or better-paying jobs. But according to researchers, even the retraining argument holds little water.Just under 30 percent of Jay’s population has a bachelor’s degree or higher, while 90 percent have a high school diploma, according to recent census data. And, as Ball State University’s Hicks says, education matters for upskilling.In the 1800s, when farm laborers were sent into the workforce in the Midwest, they had a basic education that made them trainable for factory work. Following World War II, men and women returned from the factories and the battlefield having learned many new skills, and in the post-Civil Rights South, more Black men and women became educated and entered the workforce in ways they had been unable to before.Neocloud data centers, like the one coming to Jay, can require 30 to 50 full-time staff, depending on size As Hicks points out, each of these three industrial revolutions only worked because of the big, new supply of educated people ready to move into those jobs. By contrast, the US currently has negative net immigration, low birth rates, and consistently underfunded education; there is no equivalent human-capital “wave” to support a similar jobs boom in data centers and AI, he says.“The waves of industrialization accompanied waves of human capital into the United States,” Hicks said. “So, where do we think there’s this surge of employment surrounding data centers that can mimic those three events?””The big tech companies are investing in upskilling the construction trades,” Elmo points out, citing OpenAI’s recent agreement with NABTU and noting that in fully developed data center markets, electricians and HVAC maintenance workers float from project to project as contractors. “For states that don’t have developed data center infrastructure, like Maine, it’s not a permanent job. It’s an 18-month job. That’s it.”But the tax benefitsThe jobs promise, Hicks argues, distracts from the one benefit a data center can reliably provide to a rural community: tax revenue.Using the Jay data center as an example, Hicks says that a $550 million data center in a town of 4,620 people, where the median home value is around $215,000, would carry an assessed value exceeding the combined worth of every home and every business in the town. The former mill had a tax abatement but generated roughly $1.8 million in tax revenue for Jay in its last year of operation, according to the Livermore Falls Advertiser. Taxed at the same rate as any other commercial property, that revenue could fund schools, rebuild infrastructure, and attract residents for generations.”You could make that town into a Hallmark Channel town with those sorts of tax dollars, and then jobs would follow,” Hicks said. But that all depends on whether or not the town decides to grant the future project special tax breaks.Rural towns are often “outgunned” when trying to negotiate deals with large data center buildersIt’s not clear precisely how much tax revenue the data center could generate, as of this reporting, because there are still many unknowns about the project, including who Sentinel’s clients might be, the type of data center that will be built, what kind of tax incentives the town Select Board might offer, and even how many jobs the data center might bring to Jay.McDonald’s job estimates have been all over the map, ranging from 100 to 150 as the project has progressed. He says the numbers he gave the Select Board and the state legislature are based on what Sentinel has told him, but warned that he’s “not a data center guy.”What data centers actually mean for jobsUltimately, this is a tech ouroboros. The same data center infrastructure, subsidized as a jobs program, is purpose-built to reduce human labor, and the AI it powers is explicitly designed to automate work. Communities are being asked to trade tax revenue and grid capacity for jobs in an industry whose core product is labor replacement.“It’s the biggest capital expenditure since the Manhattan Project, and it isn’t going to create tens of thousands of jobs in the long term,” Elmo said. “It’s not some economic boom. Meta, Amazon, OpenAI, Oracle, they’re shedding jobs in real time while spending billions on data centers so that other organizations can shed additional jobs through AI. At some point, people need to ask more critical questions about this.”As Hicks points out, rural America is being sold the same bill of goods it has been sold for 50 years. “Civic leaders are living 75 to 150 years in the past,” and framing these data center deals in the old industrial-boom mindset without the educational and demographic conditions that made previous booms possible.The mill didn’t save rural towns, nor did the manufacturing center, the call center, or the Amazon warehouse. Based on the economic data, data centers will not save them either, and the one thing that could genuinely help — treating the facility’s tax base as a community windfall rather than a negotiating chip — is precisely what most states are legislating away.
history.author: Abigail Bassett
history.titleZh: 数据中心正在进军美国农村
history.descriptionZh:
缅因州杰伊镇，一个距离波特兰西北约67英里的乡村小镇，其标志性的安德罗斯科金奖造纸厂在巅峰时期雇用了约1500名工人。然而，2020年的一场纸浆蒸煮器爆炸迫使这家工厂永久关闭。2023年，这座占地140万平方英尺的设施被JGT2再开发公司牵头的一家合资企业收购，开发商托尼·麦克唐纳负责该项目。在随后的三年里，麦克唐纳的团队拆除了工厂的机械设备并运往巴基斯坦，同时清理工业场地以备转售。据麦克唐纳称，转售协议已于今年初敲定——这一交易将杰伊镇推向了美国大型数据中心争夺战的最前沿。

缅因州之所以对数据中心开发商具有特殊吸引力，是因为其相对凉爽的全年气温、宽松的土地使用法规以及全美第八高的可再生能源占比（54%）。该州已有若干规划中的数据中心项目，这促使州议会最近通过一项法案，要求对任何耗电量超过20兆瓦的拟建数据中心实施为期18个月的许可证和建设暂停令。立法者希望暂停建设，以研究数据中心对地方经济、电网和环境的实际影响。然而，这项本会成为全美首个此类暂停法案的提案，上个月被缅因州州长珍妮特·米尔斯否决。她在否决理由中援引了一个压倒性的原因：就业机会。她认为，在杰伊镇这座已关闭的纸厂上建设价值5.5亿美元的设施，将为这个目睹最大雇主倒闭的小镇创造125至150个永久性的高薪职位。

从缅因州的磨坊小镇到印第安纳州的农业县，再到德克萨斯州阿比林市外的沙漠地块，数据中心开发商正告诉地方政府：接纳我们，给予我们所需，提供税收减免，就业机会就会随之而来。超过35个州已通过提供激励措施等方式来吸引该行业。然而，对于这些大型工业设施是否真的能带来它们所承诺的长期经济收益，目前的研究寥寥无几，而早期的报告则指向相反方向。专家表示，乡村社区往往缺乏必要的政府专业知识来正确评估数据中心可能对当地产生的影响。根据皮尤研究中心的最新数据，美国规划中的数据中心有67%将落户乡村地区，39%将进入目前完全没有数据中心的县。随着数据中心开发的迅速扩张，一个事实日益清晰：全国各地的乡村社区实际上得到的并非就业机会，而是一个既耗电又耗水的工业设施，其长期雇用的员工人数仅相当于一家中型餐厅。

麦克唐纳最初计划将纸厂出售给一家名为高弗雷森林产品的定向结构板公司，据称这将创造约150个就业岗位。当联邦关税导致该项目的资金支持告吹后，麦克唐纳转而考虑他一直收到的各种提议。他形容自己接到了许多来自所谓“数据中心牛仔”的电话，这些人声称拥有七大科技公司之一的客户，并正在寻找建设新数据中心的地点。但当他深入调查时，发现对方并没有其声称的坚实后盾。几次接触后，他开始与总部位于纽约的哨兵数据中心公司探讨合作。据其网站介绍，该公司专门为医疗、金融和超大规模行业服务。麦克唐纳表示，据他了解，杰伊镇的项目将是一个“新型云数据中心”，这是一种专门为人工智能和机器学习工作负载提供高性能GPU计算的设施。根据行业标准，如果这样一个数据中心进驻旧纸厂，每个机架的能耗将超过100千瓦，并且需要直接芯片冷却或浸没式冷却，这两者都需要充足的空间和水资源。

尽管麦克唐纳多次表示他对该项目的税收减免不感兴趣，但他确实希望为未来可能出现的税收优惠留下余地，如果小镇认为值得的话。就在米尔斯州长否决暂停令之前，缅因州议会通过了一项法律，将数据中心排除在该州部分商业税收减免之外，但同时也为地方市政当局提供税收减免协议和其他市政激励措施留出了空间。这可能给像杰伊这样希望通过税收意外之财来投资学校、社区建筑和道路工程的小社区带来麻烦。

缅因州暂停法案的发起人、州众议员梅兰妮·萨克斯声称，麦克唐纳直到2026年2月下旬，也就是她的暂停法案预定进行全院表决的前几天，才向杰伊镇选址委员会通报了将纸厂改建为数据中心的新计划。选址委员会在3月听取了麦克唐纳的报告，并以4比0的投票结果表示支持。暂停法案于4月14日在州众议院和参议院获得通过，米尔斯州长于4月24日否决了该法案，理由正是杰伊镇的就业创造。萨克斯表示，她的立法从未旨在禁止数据中心。“这个法案是关于制定规则手册，”她说，“我们被告知，‘别操心了，数据中心不会很快来到缅因州。’但它们还是来了，而且由于没有现成的框架，小镇没有任何机制来评估开发商的承诺。”她补充道：“就算只创造30个岗位，对杰伊镇来说意义重大，那么好吧，但这意味着为30个岗位，你牺牲了140万缅因州人的保护。”

鲍尔州立大学商业与经济研究中心主任、经济学家迈克尔·希克斯在去年11月发表了一份关于数据中心对美国就业影响的早期因果分析研究。他研究了德克萨斯州254个县的数据中心开设情况，并衡量了其对当地长期就业的实际影响。他发现，净就业创造实际上为零。数据中心存在的任何长期岗位都被同一行业的其他岗位流失所抵消。“当你开车经过一个数据中心时，你会看到有人在里面工作，”希克斯说，“你看到建筑工人，当地酒店爆满。但这没有持久效应。很多工人只是为了完成自己那部分工作而待上三周，然后就离开了……真正的问题是数据中心是否有永久性的工作岗位，在德克萨斯州，答案是没有。”德克萨斯州因其独立的电网以及大型快速发展的都市、科技中心与偏远乡村城镇并存的特点，成为理想的测试场，其经济教训可广泛应用于全美。

非营利研究机构好工作第一的安东尼·埃尔莫指出，乡村小镇在与大型数据中心开发商谈判时往往处于“火力不足”的劣势。“他们缺乏谈判资源，”埃尔莫说，“他们不知道该要求什么，没有法律专业知识，也感觉自己没有筹码，这我认为是问题的一部分。”从国家层面看，情况也不容乐观。商业内幕网最近报道，国家对数据中心的补贴每个永久性岗位的成本超过200万美元，在某些案例中，比如纽约的一个设施，公司获得了近7700万美元的税收减免，却只创造了一个永久性职位。微软位于华盛顿州昆西市的设施，规模与杰伊镇的初步计划大致相当，建设期间雇用了多达500名工人，但如今仅以50名全职员工运营。具体能创造多少长期就业岗位，取决于所建数据中心的类型。像杰伊镇这样的新型云数据中心，根据规模大小，通常需要30到50名全职员工。“在这些岗位中，很大一部分是维护人员和技术人员，负责备用发电机。高科技岗位可能只占设施全部员工的10%。”埃尔莫指出。而且很多时候，数据中心公司会将其他州的远程工作者计入该州的雇员人数。“我们可能会从中获得一点经济效应，但这远不如一个真实生活在缅因州的人购买商品、为当地经济做贡献来得多。”

关于数据中心的游说大多围绕就业创造和“技能提升”展开。但根据研究人员的说法，即使是再培训的论点也站不住脚。人口普查数据显示，杰伊镇拥有学士或以上学位的居民不到30%，而90%的人拥有高中文凭。正如希克斯所说，教育对于技能提升至关重要。在19世纪，当农场劳工进入中西部劳动力市场时，他们拥有接受工厂工作培训所需的基础教育。二战后，男女从工厂和战场归来，学到了许多新技能。而在民权运动后的南方，更多的黑人男女获得了教育并以前所未有的方式进入劳动力市场。希克斯指出，这三次工业革命之所以奏效，是因为有大量受过教育的新劳动力准备从事这些工作。相比之下，美国目前净移民为负，出生率低，教育资金持续不足；没有相应的人力资本“浪潮”来支持数据中心和人工智能领域的类似就业繁荣。“工业化的浪潮伴随着人力资本涌入美国，”希克斯说，“那么，我们凭什么认为围绕数据中心会出现能模仿那三次事件的就业激增呢？”埃尔莫指出，大型科技公司正在投资于建筑行业的技能提升，引用了OpenAI最近与北美建筑行业联合会的协议，并指出在成熟的数据中心市场，电工和暖通空调维护工人作为承包商在各个项目之间流动。“对于像缅因州这样没有成熟数据中心基础设施的州来说，这不是一份永久性工作，这是一份18个月的工作。仅此而已。”

希克斯认为，就业承诺分散了人们对数据中心能可靠提供给乡村社区的一个好处的注意力：税收收入。以杰伊数据中心为例，一个价值5.5亿美元的数据中心坐落在一个仅有4620人、家庭中位价值约21.5万美元的小镇上，其评估价值将超过镇上所有住宅和商业房产的总价值。根据当地媒体数据，前纸厂虽有税收减免，但在运营最后一年仍为杰伊镇创造了约180万美元的税收。如果以与其他商业地产相同的税率征税，这笔收入可以用于资助学校、重建基础设施并吸引居民，持续几代人。“有了这样的税收收入，你可以把那个小镇变成一个‘贺曼频道’式的美丽小镇，然后就业机会自然会随之而来。”希克斯说。但这完全取决于小镇是否决定给予未来项目特殊的税收减免。截至本文报道时，尚不清楚数据中心具体能产生多少税收，因为该项目仍有许多未知因素，包括哨兵公司的客户是谁、将建造何种类型的数据中心、镇选址委员会可能提供何种税收激励，甚至数据中心能为杰伊镇带来多少工作岗位。麦克唐纳的就业预测一直变化不定，随着项目推进，范围从100到150不等。他说他提供给选址委员会和州议会的数字是基于哨兵公司告诉他的信息，但他警告说自己“不是数据中心业内人士”。

最终，这形成了一个科技“贪食蛇”：这些作为就业计划而获得补贴的数据中心基础设施，其目的恰恰是减少人力劳动；而它们所驱动的AI，明确地被设计为自动化工作。社区被要求用税收收入和电网容量来换取一个核心产品就是替代人工的行业的就业机会。“这是自曼哈顿计划以来最大的资本支出，但它不会在长期内创造数万个工作岗位，”埃尔莫说，“这不是什么经济繁荣。Meta、亚马逊、OpenAI、甲骨文，它们在实时裁员的同时，花费数十亿美元建设数据中心，以便其他组织可以通过AI进一步削减工作岗位。在某个时候，人们需要对这个问题提出更批判性的质疑。”正如希克斯所指出的，美国乡村地区正被兜售50年来同样的一套说辞。“市政领导人活在75到150年前的过去”，以旧工业繁荣的思维定式来审视这些数据中心交易，却无视了使以往繁荣成为可能的教育和人口统计条件。造纸厂没有拯救乡村小镇，制造业中心、呼叫中心或亚马逊仓库也没有。基于经济数据，数据中心同样不能拯救它们。而唯一可能真正有所帮助的事情——将设施的税基视为社区的意外之财而非谈判筹码——恰恰是大多数州正在通过立法取消的东西。

history.titleZh: 扩展用于AI带宽的PCIe控制器：针对64 GT/s和128 GT/s的多流架构分析
history.descriptionZh: 随着PCIe迈向128 GT/s及更高速度，多流架构从一种优化选择转变为必要条件。原文《扩展PCIe控制器以满足AI带宽需求：面向64 GT/s与128 GT/s的多流架构分析》首次发表于《半导体工程》杂志。
